items.title: Government's foodgrain stocks hit 604 lakh tonnes, nearly three times buffer requirement
items.summary: India's food reserves are robust. Wheat and rice stocks in FCI godowns are nearly three times the mandatory buffer. This ensures ample supply for welfare schemes. Procurement for the next season is underway. Harvests are progressing well, with most rabi commodities trading below minimum support prices. This situation provides food security for the nation.

items.title: US hardball tactics can’t sink the push for cleaner shipping
items.summary: Countries clash over framework for regulating sector’s emissions

items.title: Standstill in India gold imports drags on, threatens supply
items.summary: Indian banks face an unprecedented five-week halt in gold and silver imports, causing domestic prices to surge and threatening shortages. Administrative hurdles and tax uncertainties have stalled shipments since April 1, impacting jewelers restocking after Akshaya Tritiya. While the India International Bullion Exchange offers an alternative, it's slower and capital-intensive.

items.title: To tax or not to tax mineral rights in India? SC to hear pleas on May 20
items.summary: The Supreme Court will hear pleas on May 20 concerning legislative power to tax mineral rights, as the Centre's curative petition is pending. Previously, a nine-judge bench ruled states hold this power, dismissing review pleas. The Centre's curative petition seeks reconsideration of this verdict.

items.title: NMDC hikes iron ore prices by Rs 200 tonne
items.summary: State-owned NMDC has raised iron ore lump and fines prices by Rs 200 per tonne, effective immediately. Baila lump ore is now priced at Rs 5,500 per tonne and fines at Rs 4,700 per tonne. This price hike, exclusive of various taxes and fees, will impact steel manufacturing costs.

items.title: Campaigners call for ban on use of weedkiller glyphosate at harvest time
items.summary: Campaigners are calling for a ban on the use of the weedkiller over health concerns.

items.title: Apollo chief says retail fund mark-ups ‘make no sense’
items.summary: Marc Rowan criticises paper returns of popular private equity funds sold to retail investors

items.title: India’s exports rise 4.6% to hit all-time high of $863 bn in FY26 despite global headwinds
items.summary: India's export landscape witnessed an extraordinary transformation in 2025-26, as figures climbed to an astounding USD 863.11 billion. Amidst a backdrop of challenging global economic conditions, the country demonstrated resilience with a slight gain in merchandise exports. However, the real standout was the services sector, which saw exports reach an exhilarating peak.

items.title: Managers, get your hands dirty or get fired: Lessons from AI-powered tech layoffs at Coinbase, Block, Snap
items.summary: As layoffs become the law of the land, and artificial intelligence (AI) drives productivity, it is managers that are becoming redundant.

items.summary: No extra price rises are slated for the UK, which saw better than expected sales in the first quarter.

items.title: United Breweries flags ₹400–500 crore cost hit as margins come under pressure
items.summary: The Heineken-controlled brewer reported a decline in FY26 revenue and profit as packaging costs surged and pricing delays offset steady demand and premium growth.

items.title: ADB warns Asian economies against broad fuel subsidies, tax cuts amid oil price risks
items.summary: The Asian Development Bank advised Asian economies to avoid broad fuel subsidies and excise duty cuts, warning they could strain public finances amid elevated crude oil prices. The lender suggested targeted cash transfers for vulnerable populations and a shift to green energy, while urging central banks to monitor inflation expectations before tightening monetary policy.

items.title: Mphasis sues Coforge in US court over employee hiring, client exposure claims
items.summary: Blackstone-owned Mphasis has alleged that rival Coforge recruited its employees in breach of contractual restrictions and gained access to confidential client information tied to Charles Schwab, intensifying scrutiny over non-compete enforcement in India’s IT services sector.

items.title: Private credit’s $2 trillion boom raises global stability fears, watchdog warns
items.summary: The Financial Stability Board wants regulators to tighten up their supervision of private credit amid fears of growing stress in the sector.

items.summary: OpenAI president Greg Brockman spoke during the second week of a month-long trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI's Sam Altman.

items.title: How India’s growth story has left its workers behind
items.summary: Noida’s unrest highlights India’s structural crisis: skipping manufacturing for services has left a low-skilled workforce with stagnant real wages, poor living and working conditions, and diminishing bargaining power.

items.title: Resilient, not shock-free: India charts path through war jitters
items.summary: India's economy faces challenges from the West Asia conflict. Rising oil prices and trade disruptions are impacting India. However, strong domestic demand and stable financial conditions provide resilience. The government and Reserve Bank of India are taking steps to manage risks. India is better prepared than in past crises. Structural reforms are key for future growth.

items.title: A 1% problem that can derail India’s factory lines as tiny screws throw big tantrums
items.summary: A recent analysis highlights that stringent regulations on fasteners, a seemingly minor expense, are leading to significant production hurdles. These limitations are driving up costs and limiting available supply, adversely affecting sectors such as automotive and construction. The 'Make in India' initiative is under considerable threat. The report advocates for a more straightforward, risk-focused approach for essential safety components.

items.title: Aadhar Housing targets 20% growth in FY27, eyes ₹50,000 crore AUM in three years
items.summary: The Blackstone-backed lender said West Asia tensions have no material impact on loan book as it sees steady demand from affordable housing and state-backed projects.

items.title: Facebook founder's venture firm to step up investments in India's deeptech, AI and robotics sectors
items.summary: B Capital, led by Eduardo Saverin, plans to invest $50-$100 million annually. The firm, which manages over $10 billion in assets, sees potential in India's growing market, particularly in AI and robotics.

items.title: India’s services sector recovers lost ground, domestic market fuels growth in April: HSBC India Services PMI
items.summary: India's services sector showed recovery in April, with the HSBC PMI rising to 58.8. Increased output and new business orders were noted, alongside a shift to domestic suppliers. The composite PMI also rose to 58.2, indicating growth momentum across sectors.

items.title: India services growth picks up in April on strong domestic demand, PMI shows
items.summary: India's services sector saw growth in April, boosted by strong domestic demand. This rise followed a dip in March. However, global orders slowed. Middle East uncertainties are impacting future optimism. New business accelerated, with consumer services leading the expansion. International sales growth weakened. Operating expenses rose at a softer pace. Employment expanded at the strongest pace in ten months.

items.title: In the global AI race, a sanctioned Chinese firm says cheaper models can still win
items.summary: As China’s AI competition intensifies, SenseTime is shifting toward multimodal AI, lower-cost models and overseas expansion to remain competitive, co-founder Lin Dahua said.

items.title: Veritas Finance explores ₹1,000 crore secondary deal as IPO plans delay
items.summary: Chennai-based NBFC considers ₹800–1,000 crore largely secondary deal to offer exits to early investors as volatile markets delay its IPO plans.

items.title: US Envoy Sergio Gor confirms Marco Rubio will visit India soon to ‘build even stronger ties’
items.summary: US envoy Sergio Gor announced that Indian companies plan to invest over $20.5 billion in various sectors, contributing to job creation and stronger supply chains. The SelectUSA Investment Summit promotes foreign direct investment, connecting global business leaders with US economic developers.

items.summary: Samsung Electronics Co. has reached a $1 trillion market valuation after booming demand for chips used in artificial intelligence saw the world’s largest memory maker’s stock more than quadruple over the past year.

items.summary: Coal India has always been known for its strong dividend yield. We analyse the prospects of the company in the next few years.

items.summary: US Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau stated that the US and India are "very, very close" to signing a trade deal, needing to overcome a "last hurdle." Negotiations have been ongoing for months, with both sides aiming to finalize the agreement and address other bilateral issues.

items.summary: Once India’s top smartphone brand, Xiaomi has slipped out of the top five as rivals dominate a market reshaped by premiumization, retail expansion,and shifting consumer demand.

items.title: West Asia war may briefly dent India's growth as fundamentals strong: Memani
items.summary: CII President Rajiv Memani, who is also the regional managing partner of EY Africa-India and chair of EY Growth Markets Council, said that the challenge before businesses is to strike a balance between the costs that they can absorb and those that must be passed on to consumers.

items.title: Why Google DeepMind workers in UK are trying to unionise over company's Pentagon AI deals
items.summary: Google DeepMind employees in the UK have voted to unionise, demanding an end to Google's Pentagon AI deal and military contracts. Here is what the landmark bid means for the future of AI ethics in the workplace.

items.title: Apple to pay up to $95 to some US iPhone buyers over AI lawsuit
items.summary: Claims from last year said the tech firm’s advertising of Apple Intelligence fooled iPhone buyers.

items.summary: President Donald Trump said he is pausing "Project Freedom," the U.S. military's bid to guide commercial ships out of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Sugarcane FRP hiked to Rs 365/quintal for 2026-27 season
items.summary: Farmers will receive a premium of ₹3.56 per quintal for every 0.1% increase in recovery above this level, with a corresponding reduction for lower recovery.

items.title: Credit growth signals economic strength: Finance Ministry
items.summary: Non-food credit growth in FY26 stood at 15.9% against 10.9% in the corresponding period last year. The aggregate credit outstanding in March 2026 reached ₹212.9 lakh crore, ₹29.2 lakh crore higher than the previous year.

items.title: Rare earths, PCBs among 40 sub-sectors on FDI fast track
items.summary: As per the updated standard operating procedure (SOP) for processing FDI applications, the government has also rolled out reporting guidelines for investments in India from a company having any direct or indirect ownership by a citizen or an entity of a country sharing a land border with India.

items.title: Iran war could drag India's GDP growth to 6.5% this fiscal, says CII president Rajiv Memani
items.summary: India's economic growth forecast for this fiscal year is around 7 percent. This projection depends on the Iran war ending soon. If the conflict persists, growth could slow to 6.5 percent. Companies are building buffers against future shocks. Private investments are expected to rebound once the West Asia conflict subsides. Consumption demand remains strong.

items.title: Nepal to procure 80,000 tonnes of fertiliser from India as Iran disrupts global supply chains
items.summary: Nepal will purchase 80,000 tonnes of fertilizer from India through a government-to-government deal to mitigate the impact of global supply disruptions and rising prices on its farm output. This one-time purchase aims to address a projected shortfall for the upcoming paddy transplantation season, as escalating global costs have reduced Nepal's overall import capacity.

items.title: Moody's flags India as most resilient emerging market since 2020
items.summary: India is a strong emerging market, managing global shocks effectively since 2020. Early policy reforms and robust buffers are key. Predictable monetary policy and inflation targeting have anchored expectations. Large foreign exchange reserves stabilize currency and boost investor confidence during global stress. India's resilience is a significant development.

items.title: Fresh AI Disruption Selloff Hits Thomson Reuters Despite Strong Results
items.summary: Thomson Reuters Corp. shares fell despite positive first-quarter results after Anthropic PBC unveiled new artificial intelligence agents designed to handle a broader mix of financial services tasks.

items.title: Tata Power’s Bhutan hydro project gets $515 mn World Bank nod
items.summary: The project in eastern Bhutan, once completed, is set to generate 4,500GWh of clean electricity every year, with 80% of it to be exported to India during the peak summer season.

items.title: Govt clears ₹50,000-crore package to boost credit flow and support businesses
items.summary: The government-backed loan support scheme will facilitate an additional credit flow of up to ₹2.55 trillion to the economy, including ₹5,000 crore specifically for the aviation sector, a government statement said.

items.title: Lidl's new loyalty scheme less generous, shoppers say
items.summary: Under the changed system customers collect points rather than reward coupons, with £1 spent equalling one point.

items.title: UK long-term borrowing costs reach 28-year high
items.summary: There have been extra jitters in UK government debt markets ahead of Thursday's local and national elections.

items.title: India, Jamaica agree to boost trade, skilled mobility: Jaishankar
items.summary: India and Jamaica are strengthening trade and cooperation for skilled professionals. Agreements were signed in health, solarisation, and broadcasting. Discussions covered digital transformation, cultural exchange, and digital payments. Both nations will enhance business and investment ties. Cooperation will expand in defence, security, healthcare, digitisation, agriculture, education, and infrastructure. India also provided medical units and dialysis machines.

items.title: NSE's profit falls 15% in FY26 as market activity moderates
items.summary: A cooling derivatives market and a massive ₹1,391-crore provision for Sebi settlements weighed on annual earnings, even as a strong March quarter provided a boost ahead of the exchange's planned IPO.

items.title: War-driven inflation dents L&T's margin, company warns the effects may persist in FY27
items.summary: As part of its new five-year plan, dubbed Lakshya 31, the company is aiming for 12-15% compounded annual growth in its revenues between FY26 and FY31. That translates to a top line of ₹5.8 trillion by FY31 at the upper end, double of FY26.

items.title: Nissan to close UK line and cut 900 European jobs
items.summary: Nissan says it is considering working with a third party to fully utilise its Sunderland plant.

items.title: SCBs clock 15.9% credit growth in FY26, led by services, MSMEs, retail
items.summary: The 497-basis-point jump reflects sustained economic momentum and rising demand for credit across sectors.

items.title: Centre okays sunflower seed procurement in Karnataka, extends purchase window for gram in Maharashtra
items.summary: The central government has approved the purchase of sunflower seed from Karnataka for the 2026 rabi season. This move ensures farmers receive a minimum support price. In Maharashtra, the procurement limit for gram has been significantly increased.

items.title: What's happening to UK petrol and diesel prices?
items.summary: Motoring group RAC has said prices at the pump could keep rising if there is no resolution to the Iran war.

items.title: RBI releases norms for banks holding non financial assets
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has released new draft rules. Banks can now hold specific non-financial assets only to recover bad loans. These assets must be sold within seven years. Selling them back to borrowers is prohibited. This aims for transparency and better recovery. The rules are open for public comment until May 26.

items.title: Inflation target can be cut if growth, stable prices persist: RBI deputy governor
items.summary: India might lower its inflation target and narrow the tolerance band. This consideration hinges on sustained robust economic growth and stable inflation over the next five years. The Reserve Bank of India's Deputy Governor Poonam Gupta highlighted this possibility. However, persistent global challenges may necessitate retaining the current framework's flexibility. The final decision will depend on evolving economic outcomes.

items.title: Sustained reforms will help India maintain growth momentum, says ADB’s Albert Park
items.summary: India's economy shows strong growth prospects despite global challenges. The Asian Development Bank highlights the need for continued reforms to enhance business and trade. Inflation remains a key concern, potentially impacting interest rate decisions. Strategic focus on renewable energy and targeted subsidies will bolster resilience. India is poised to benefit from AI advancements with proper skill development.

items.title: Government specifies 40 sub-sectors for faster clearance of FDI proposals from land-bordering nations
items.summary: India is fast-tracking foreign investment proposals from China and neighboring nations. Forty key manufacturing sub-sectors, including rare earth magnets and electronics, will now receive clearances within 60 days. This move aims to boost domestic production. However, Indian citizens must maintain majority ownership and control in these ventures. New reporting guidelines are also in place for investments from these countries.

items.title: Gap co-founder Doris Fisher dies aged 94
items.summary: She opened the first store with her husband Don in 1969, with the company calling her "a pioneering force in American retail".

items.title: India plans to award construction-ready highway contracts, with all approvals in place, to curb delays
items.summary: In a bid to eliminate persistent highway project delays, MoRTH is proposing stricter pre-award filters, ensuring all critical land and structural clearances are in place before contracts are awarded. This shift aims to make highway projects more execution-ready.

items.title: Why RBI wants to keep India's gold at home
items.summary: RBI repatriated over 100 tonnes of gold to India in the six months to March, taking the total gold reserves stored locally to 680 tonnes. Why is the central bank bringing gold back home?

items.title: India among most resilient as global shocks test emerging markets: Moody’s Ratings
items.summary: India and Thailand are well placed to manage future shocks because monetary policy frameworks are clear and predictable, inflation expectations are well anchored, and exchange rates can adjust when needed, Moody’s Ratings said.
